As I spent a lots of money and got nothing and not even an answer it is very annoying and frustrating. Either should be getting rid of the page as it’s probably scam or start to get the orders sorted as daily receive emails from them about deals but nothing about my order. results.title: Google | About this store
results.content:
How the company could improve:
FunnyFuzzy needs to provide basic order tracking, transparent billing, and timely customer support responses. Clear communication about shipping timelines, prompt explanations of billing discrepancies, and honest engagement on social media would have prevented most of this frustration. Product quality also needs SIGNIFICANT improvement to match the price.
Based on my experience, I would not recommend this company.
Please note... I DID NOT receive a REFUND until my credit card got involved and removed the charges while I was on the phone with them. FunnyFuzzy did NOT provide the refund. [...] Then when we finally got our purchase after many many conversations, the purchase stinks. They don't stay in place and they don't fit the couch. When asked for the measurements of the couch I measured the couch. I didn't measure for anything to be tucked in because I assumed there would be an extra couple inches on each piece. Those stupid squares that are supposed to go in the back of the couch never stay in place. They are useless.
How's that? I don't like your product and I don't like your customer service. I would never ever recommend funny fuzzy to everyone. [...] I ordered a couch cover from FunnyFuzzy and waited over three months with no tracking updates or delivery communication. After six weeks, customer service said they couldn’t provide tracking because the item was shipping by ocean. I also discovered an extra charge on my credit card despite placing only one order and receiving a receipt for just one. Repeated emails asking about the charges and delivery timeline went largely unanswered.

results.title: Why Customer Service Fails: It’s the System, Not the People
results.content:
My point is that I hear so many people complaining about customer service, but in many cases, it’s not the people. It’s the system. Some of the reasons customers complain about customer service or experience due to broken or poorly created systems are: 

1. Hard-to-find contact information for customer support – While most websites provide information about customer support, sometimes it’s not clearly visible. When a customer wants to contact you, don’t add to their frustration by making it difficult.  

2. Inability to connect with someone from customer support – Some companies are still betting that a 100% AI-fueled self-service option can replace live agents. In almost every case, they are wrong! [...] with the product, but my rep, she remained calm and did everything in her power to resolve the issue. She gets an A for effort, an A for attitude, and an A for the knowledge that she demonstrated. Anyway, my point is that I hear so many people complaining about customer service. And it turns out that in many cases, it is not the people, it is the system. Some, not all of the reasons customers complain about customer service or experience due to a broken or poorly created system are, and I'm giving you a number of them, seven of them. Number one, hard to find contact information for customer support. So, while most websites provide information about customer support, sometimes it's not obviously visible. When a customer wants to contact you, don't add to their frustration by making it [...] 3. Self-service options are not provided – Just as some companies make the error of going “all in” on digital self-service support, some companies don’t provide any self-service options. There is a percentage of customers who demand the option, or they will choose to do business elsewhere. 

4. Self-service options are provided, but they don’t answer the question or offer a solution – While this alone can be frustrating, imagine if the self-service is not working and the company makes it difficult or impossible to connect to a live person. This is adding fuel to the fire.
